channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Opinion: Is democracy a joke? Count Binface says no.
item.description: Populist British MP Nigel Farage resigned from Parliament over questions about his finances, and is running for re-election in his constituency. His biggest rival? Count Binface.
item.content:encoded:
Populist British MP Nigel Farage resigned from Parliament over questions about his finances, and is running for re-election in his constituency. His biggest rival? Count Binface.

(Image credit: BENJAMIN CREMEL/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Most people who need glasses don't have them. Can the post office change that?
item.description: In some towns in India, a visitor to the post office who's squinting at fine print might be asked: Do you want an eye test?
item.content:encoded:
In some towns in India, a visitor to the post office who's squinting at fine print might be asked: Do you want an eye test?

(Image credit: Subhamoy Bhattacharjee for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Minnesota is pulling troops early from D.C., as pressure grows on Michigan
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz is pulling his National Guard early from Washington, D.C. as the chorus against Democratic governors sending troops to the city amid President Trump's ongoing deployment grows louder.
item.content:encoded:
Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz is pulling his National Guard early from Washington, D.C. as the chorus against Democratic governors sending troops to the city amid President Trump's ongoing deployment grows louder.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: Shy on the dance floor? Virtual reality 'partners' aim to help you find your groove
item.description: VR dance lesson apps like Dance Guru and Trip the Light offer a judgment-free way to learn partner dancing.
item.content:encoded:
VR dance lesson apps like Dance Guru and Trip the Light offer a judgment-free way to learn partner dancing.

(Image credit: Chloe Veltman)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: With the white nationalist group Patriot Front, what you see is not what you get
item.description: Hundreds of masked white nationalists marched in the nation's capital on July Fourth. Who were they and where does their funding come from?
item.content:encoded:
Hundreds of masked white nationalists marched in the nation's capital on July Fourth. Who were they and where does their funding come from?

(Image credit: Cheney Orr)
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef

item.title: Spain edges Belgium and will face France in World Cup semifinal
item.description: Spain and France will meet Tuesday in Arlington, Texas, in a matchup anticipated for years. Neither team has lost at this year's World Cup.
item.content:encoded:
Spain and France will meet Tuesday in Arlington, Texas, in a matchup anticipated for years. Neither team has lost at this year's World Cup.

(Image credit: Mark J. Terrill)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump threatens Iran after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral saw calls for his killing
item.description: Trump made the comments on his Truth Social after U.S. officials demanded that Iran make a public statement saying the Strait of Hormuz is open.
item.content:encoded:
Trump made the comments on his Truth Social after U.S. officials demanded that Iran make a public statement saying the Strait of Hormuz is open.

(Image credit: Iranian Supreme Leader Office)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump administration rolls back a key protection for imperiled wildlife
item.description: The Trump administration finalized a rule Friday that changes how agencies enforce the Endangered Species Act. The administration narrowed the definition of "harm" under the landmark law.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration finalized a rule Friday that changes how agencies enforce the Endangered Species Act. The administration narrowed the definition of "harm" under the landmark law.

(Image credit: Carolyn Kaster)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Graham Platner makes it official in Maine, submitting paperwork to leave Senate race
item.description: In his withdrawal notice, Platner said "people are desperate for change" and that's why they made him the Democratic nominee. Now, Maine Democrats have to pick someone to replace him by July 27.
item.content:encoded:
In his withdrawal notice, Platner said "people are desperate for change" and that's why they made him the Democratic nominee. Now, Maine Democrats have to pick someone to replace him by July 27.

(Image credit: CJ Gunther)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: Patriotic art gets the spotlight as NEA funding shifts. Cue 'The Ronald Reagan Overture'
item.description: Patriotic art and music is taking center stage this year under the Trump Administration, as funds shift away from DEI. For some orgs, like the Reagan Presidential Library, this is their wheelhouse.
item.content:encoded:
Patriotic art and music is taking center stage this year under the Trump Administration, as funds shift away from DEI. For some orgs, like the Reagan Presidential Library, this is their wheelhouse.

(Image credit: Consolidated News Pictures/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman
